docs.title: Gophish-NG
docs.text: Gophish-NG is a fork of the open-source Gophish phishing simulation framework, extended with additional capabilities for red team engagements.

docs.title: What's new in Gophish-NG?
docs.text: Feature Description Attachment Tracking Track when a recipient executes a delivered payload Password-Protected ZIP Send encrypted ZIP attachments with per-template passwords IOC Removal Replace Gophish-specific headers and parameters Script support .ps1, .bat, .pdf files support placeholder substitution Campaign Results "Clicked Attachment" and "Email Reported" status in results table

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/OppressionBreedsResistance/gophish-ng.git
cd gophish-ng
go build
./gophish

Open https://localhost:3333 in your browser. Login credentials are printed in the console on first run.

Authorized Use Only

Gophish-NG is intended for authorized security assessments, red team engagements, and security awareness training only.

docs.title: Attachment Tracking
docs.text: Gophish-NG can track when a recipient executes the delivered payload by beaconing back to the phishing server.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. The payload script sends an HTTP request to /attachment?keyname={{.RId}} on execution
  2. Gophish-NG records a Clicked Attachment event for that recipient
  3. If the email had not been marked as opened yet, the open event is automatically inferred

docs.title: Setting Up the Beacon
docs.text:
Add the following to your PowerShell payload:

Invoke-WebRequest -Uri "{{.URL}}/attachment?keyname={{.RId}}" -UseBasicParsing | Out-Null

For batch scripts:

powershell -Command "Invoke-WebRequest -Uri '{{.URL}}/attachment?keyname={{.RId}}' -UseBasicParsing | Out-Null"

docs.title: Results
docs.text:
The Clicked Attachment event appears:

  • As a purple status label in the campaign results table
  • As a purple donut chart in the campaign results view
  • In the event timeline for the recipient

Tip

The beacon URL uses {{.URL}} which already includes the phishing server address. The {{.RId}} placeholder is the unique recipient identifier automatically substituted by Gophish-NG.

docs.title: IOC Removal
docs.text: Gophish-NG removes or replaces indicators of compromise (IOCs) that are present in the original Gophish and can be used to fingerprint the framework.

docs.title: Changes
docs.text: What Original value New value Email header X-Gophish-Contact X-Contact Webhook header X-Gophish-Signature X-Signature Server name / X-Mailer gophish (omitted) Recipient URL parameter rid keyname 404 response Go default page Custom page

docs.title: URL Parameter Change
docs.text:
Tracking links use ?keyname=... instead of ?rid=....

Warning

If you are using custom landing pages or external tooling that reads the rid parameter, update them to use keyname instead.

docs.title: Password-Protected ZIP Attachments
docs.text: Gophish-NG supports sending ZIP archives encrypted with ZipCrypto as email attachments, with placeholder substitution applied to files inside.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. You create a ZIP file with ZipCrypto encryption containing your payload
  2. Gophish-NG decrypts the archive on send, applies placeholder substitution to supported files, and re-encrypts before attaching to the email
  3. Each recipient receives a personalized payload inside a password-protected archive

docs.title: 1. Create the payload
docs.text:
Create your script with placeholders, e.g. payload.ps1:

$url = "{{.URL}}"
$name = "{{.FirstName}}"
Invoke-WebRequest -Uri "$url/attachment?keyname={{.RId}}" -UseBasicParsing | Out-Null

docs.title: 2. Create a password-protected ZIP
docs.text:
Use ZipCrypto encryption (default in most tools):

7-Zip GUI7-Zip CLI
  1. Right-click the file → 7-Zip → Add to archive
  2. Archive format: zip
  3. Encryption method: ZipCrypto
  4. Enter password
7z a -tzip -p"yourpassword" archive.zip payload.ps1

Use ZipCrypto, not AES-256

Gophish-NG supports ZipCrypto encryption only. Do not use AES-256 (-mem=AES256 in 7-Zip), as it is not supported and will result in a broken attachment.

docs.title: 3. Attach in Gophish-NG
docs.text:
1. Go to Email Templates → New/Edit Template
  2. Attach the .zip file
  3. A Password field appears next to the attachment — enter the ZIP password
  4. Save the template

docs.title: 4. Result
docs.text:
Each recipient receives the .zip with personalized content inside, protected by the password you set.

Tip

The password is the same for all recipients. The personalization happens inside the archive — each recipient's .ps1 contains their unique {{.RId}} and other substituted values.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Gophish-NG uses a config.json file in the root directory.

{
  "admin_server": {
    "listen_url": "0.0.0.0:3333",
    "use_tls": true,
    "cert_path": "gophish_admin.crt",
    "key_path": "gophish_admin.key"
  },
  "phish_server": {
    "listen_url": "0.0.0.0:80",
    "use_tls": false
  },
  "db_name": "sqlite3",
  "db_path": "gophish.db",
  "migrations_prefix": "db/db_",
  "contact_address": "",
  "logging": {
    "filename": "",
    "level": ""
  }
}

docs.title: Key Settings
docs.text: Key Description admin_server.listen_url Address and port for the admin panel phish_server.listen_url Address and port for the phishing server (landing pages, tracking) db_name Database driver: sqlite3 or mysql db_path Path to the SQLite database file contact_address Email address embedded in outgoing emails as X-Contact header

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text: • Go v1.10 or above

docs.title: Build from Source
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/OppressionBreedsResistance/gophish-ng.git
cd gophish-ng
go build

docs.title: Running
docs.text:
./gophish

On first run, login credentials are printed to the console:

time="..." level=info msg="Please login with the username admin and the password 4304d5255378177d"

Open https://localhost:3333 in your browser.

docs.title: Attachments
docs.text: Gophish-NG extends attachment support with placeholder substitution and password-protected ZIP files.

docs.title: Supported File Types
docs.text:
Extension Placeholder Substitution Notes .txt Yes Plain text .html Yes HTML files .ps1 Yes PowerShell scripts .bat Yes Batch scripts .pdf Yes* *Plain-text streams only — see note below .zip Yes (contents) Unpacked, substituted, repacked

PDF Limitation

PDF substitution works only if the placeholder text is stored as plain text in the PDF content stream. PDFs with compressed streams (zlib/deflate) will not be processed correctly and may become corrupted.

For best results, export PDFs from Word using default settings, which typically does not compress text streams.

docs.title: ZIP Attachments
docs.text:
When a .zip file is used as an attachment, Gophish-NG:

  1. Decrypts the archive (if password-protected)
  2. Applies template substitution to supported file types inside
  3. Repacks and re-encrypts the archive before sending

See Password-Protected ZIP for full setup instructions.

docs.title: Adding an Attachment
docs.text:
1. Go to Email Templates → New/Edit Template
  2. Scroll to the Attachments section
  3. Click Add Files and select your file
  4. For ZIP files, a Password field appears — enter the ZIP password if applicable
  5. Save the template

docs.title: Campaign Results
docs.text: The campaign results view has been extended with two additional status indicators.

docs.title: Status Levels
docs.text: Status Color Description Email Sent Green Email successfully delivered Email Opened Yellow Recipient opened the email Clicked Link Orange Recipient clicked the phishing link Submitted Data Red Recipient submitted data on the landing page Clicked Attachment Purple Recipient executed the payload Email Reported Cyan Recipient reported the email as suspicious

docs.title: Clicked Attachment
docs.text: Tracked when the payload beacons back to the phishing server. See Attachment Tracking.

docs.title: Email Reported
docs.text: Displayed as a label in the results table when a recipient reports the email. This status does not affect the sequential event progression — it is an independent flag shown alongside the primary status.

docs.title: Donut Charts
docs.text: The results page shows one donut chart per status. Clicked Attachment appears in purple, matching the status label color.

docs.title: Campaigns
docs.text:
Campaigns in Gophish-NG work the same as upstream Gophish. A campaign ties together:

  • A sending profile (SMTP server)
  • An email template (with optional attachments)
  • A landing page
  • A target group (list of recipients)

docs.title: Creating a Campaign
docs.text:
1. Go to Campaigns → New Campaign
  2. Fill in the campaign name, email template, landing page, URL, and sending profile
  3. Select a target group
  4. Set a launch date (or launch immediately)
  5. Click Launch Campaign

docs.title: Monitoring Results
docs.text: Click the chart icon next to a campaign to open the results view. See Campaign Results for details on the status indicators.

docs.title: Email Templates
docs.text: Email templates work the same as in upstream Gophish, with the addition of attachment support.

docs.title: Available Placeholders
docs.text:
Placeholder Description {{.FirstName}} Recipient's first name {{.LastName}} Recipient's last name {{.Position}} Recipient's position/title {{.Email}} Recipient's email address {{.From}} Sender address {{.URL}} Tracking/phishing URL {{.RId}} Unique recipient ID

These placeholders work in the email body, subject, and inside supported attachment files (.ps1, .bat, .pdf, and files inside .zip archives).

docs.title: Tracking URL
docs.text:
The {{.URL}} placeholder expands to the phishing server URL with the recipient's unique identifier:

https://your-server.com/?keyname=RECIPIENT_ID

Note

Gophish-NG uses keyname instead of rid as the URL parameter. Update any landing pages or external tooling accordingly.
